mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 1.5%) signals deflationary risks that prompt central banks to maintain accommodative policies, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 60%+ of periods when CPIAUCSL < 1.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 4.0% for 3 months) signals tight labor markets that pressure wages and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when UNRATE falls > 0.5pp in 3 months, or the return dispersion in these periods exceeds 15%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS > 0.5% MoM) and housing starts (HOUST > 1.0% MoM) signals robust demand conditions that drive S&P 500 forward returns positively over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when both RSAFS and HOUST exceed thresholds, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO < 0.3% MoM) signal weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.3% for 2 months, or the return volatility in these periods exceeds 25%.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in payroll employment (PAYEMS > 200k MoM) amid rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.5%) signals labor market disequilibrium that drives S&P 500 returns negatively over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 50%+ of instances when PAYEMS > 200k and UNRATE > 4.5%, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1 < 0.5% QoQ) signals economic slowdown, prompting risk-off behavior that drives S&P 500 forward returns negatively over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when GDPC1 declines > 0.5% QoQ, or the return dispersion in these periods exceeds 20%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) signals monetary tightening that raises equity discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 1 month, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns drops below -0.15.
